Application For Default Judgment By Court
Off Calendar Aug-18-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing as to defendant Bradley D. Carrier.
Dept. 514. Judge: Gerardo C. Sandoval. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty. Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR #8098. Telephone: 415-551-3680. Email: gcasey@sftc.org.

Kenneth R. Wachtel, Esq. appeared via CourtCall for plaintiff Zions Bancorporation, N.A. dba California Bank & Trust.
Lila Colridge, Vice President of Zions Bancorporation, N.A., was placed under oath and testified.
Ms. Colridge appeared via CourtCall for today's proceedings.
No appearance by defendant Bradley D. Carrier.
Plaintiff's Complaint for Breach of Contract, Open Book Account, Money Had and Received, Account Stated and Money Lent was filed on October 16, 2019, and was personally served on defendant Bradley D. Carrier on October 27, 2019.
Default as to defendant Bradley D. Carrier was entered on January 24, 2020.
DOE defendants were dismissed on May 8, 2020.
Plaintiff's Memorandum of Costs was filed on May 8, 2020.

Plaintiff Zions Bancorporation, N.A. submits the following exhibit for the Court's review:
Exhibit 1: Declaration of Kenneth R. Wachtel filed May 8, 2020........................ID/EVID

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of plaintiff Zions Bancorporation, N.A. dba California Bank & Trust, and against the defaulted defendant Bradley D. Carrier, as follows:

Damages/Principal - $99,668.72.
Prejudgment Interest - $11,684.40.
Attorney Fees - $13,854.00.
Costs - 699.65.

Total - $125,906.77.

Counsel for plaintiff shall prepare the proposed Judgment and submit same to the Court for review and signature.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-04-2020 Continued To Jul-15-20 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service And Complaint For Lack Of Subject Matter Jurisdiction
Off Calendar Dec-23-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

DEFENDANT BRADLEY CARRIER's Motion To Quash Service And Complaint For Lack Of Subject Matter Jurisdiction.

Denied. "[S]uperior courts are courts of general subject matter jurisdiction and can adjudicate any and all cases before them." (Long v. Forty Niners Football (2019) 33 Cal.App.5th 550, 556.) Thus, this court has subject matter jurisdiction even if there is also subject matter jurisdiction in federal court.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service And Complaint For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction
Off Calendar Dec-23-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

DEFENDANT BRADLEY CARRIER's Motion To Quash Service And Complaint For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction.

Denied. There is a mandatory forum selection clause in Plaintiff and Defendant's loan agreement by which Plaintiff agreed on request to submit to the jurisdiction of the courts of San Francisco County. "Forum selection clauses are valid and may be given effect." (Miller-Leigh v. Henson (2007) 152 Cal.App.1143, 1149.) Thus, this court has personal jurisdiction over Defendant.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-23-2019 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:

DEFENDANT BRADLEY CARRIER's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.

Overruled. The demurrer to the first cause of action is overruled because the loan agreement is attached as exhibit A to the complaint and therefore it can be "ascertained from the pleading" that the contract is a written contract, per 430.10(g). The demurrers to the second, third, fourth, and fifth causes of action are overruled because it is a well-established principle that a complaint can allege multiple causes of action, pled in the alternative. (See Mendoza v. Cont'l Sales Co. (2006) 140 Cal.App.4th 1395, 1402.)

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Complaint
Off Calendar Dec-23-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

DEFENDANT BRADLEY CARRIER's Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Complaint.

Denied. Under Code of Civ. Proc. 413.10(b) summons on someone outside of California shall be served "as provided in this chapter or as prescribed by the law of the place where the person is served." Under 415.40, "summons may be served on a person outside this state in any manner provided by this article." One of the ways provided for in the Code of Civ. Proc. under 415.10 is "by personal delivery of a copy of the summons and of the complaint to the person to be served. Service of a summons in this manner is deemed complete at the time of such delivery." Defendant was personally served and thus service was complete in accordance with California law.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss On The Grounds Of Fraud In The Inducement
Off Calendar Dec-23-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

DEFENDANT BRADLEY CARRIER's Motion To Dismiss On The Grounds Of Fraud In The Inducement.

Denied. What Defendant is asserting is an affirmative defense, not proper on a motion to dismiss/demurrer. "There is no statutory authority for using a motion to dismiss as a method to challenge opposing pleadings. Under the Code, dismissal motions are expressly authorized only on specified grounds." (Cal. Prac. Guide Civ. Pro. Before Trial Ch. 7(I)-D, 7:370.) Demurrers are similarly limited to the grounds specified in Code fo Civ. Proc. 430.10. Such an argument should be raised as an affirmative defense in D's answer, provided for in Code of Civ. Proc. 431.30(g). Fraud is an affirmative defense to be raised in a contract action. (Cal. Prac. Guide Civ. Pro. Before Trial Ch. 6-C, 6:439.)

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Further Bill Of Particulars
Off Calendar Dec-23-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

DEFENDANT BRADLEY CARRIER's Motion For Further Bill Of Particulars.

Denied. "For many purposes a bill of particulars, when made in response to a statutory demand, is an amplification of the complaint. It makes plaintiff's demand more specific, and limits his proof to the items set out." (Edelman v. McDonnell (1899) 126 Cal.210, 213.) Defendant's request prior to litigation, prior to becoming an adverse party or there being demands in need of further specification does not constitute the demand contemplated under Code of Civ. Proc. 454.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-23-2019 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:

DEFENDANT BRADLEY CARRIER's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.

Overruled. "A demurrer lies for lack of standing when the defect appears on the face of the pleading or from judicially noticeable matters." (Qualified Patients Assn. v. City of Anaheim (2010) 187 Cal.App.4th 734, 752.) That Zions is not the real party in interest and thus has no standing to sue is not apparent from the face of the pleading nor from matters judicially noticeable.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss For Lack Of Exhaustion
Off Calendar Dec-23-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

DEFENDANT BRADLEY CARRIER's Motion And Motion To Dismiss For Lack Of Exhaustion.

Denied. What Defendant is asserting is an affirmative defense, not proper on a motion to dismiss/demurrer. "There is no statutory authority for using a motion to dismiss as a method to challenge opposing pleadings. Under the Code, dismissal motions are expressly authorized only on specified grounds." (Cal. Prac. Guide Civ. Pro. Before Trial Ch. 7(I)-D, 7:370.) Demurrers are similarly limited to the grounds specified in Code fo Civ. Proc. 430.10. Such an argument should be raised as an affirmative defense in D's answer, provided for in Code of Civ. Proc. 431.30(g).

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-23-2019 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:

DEFENDANT BRADLEY CARRIER's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.

Overruled. A "demurrer for uncertainty is strictly construed, even where a complaint is in some respects uncertain, because ambiguities can be clarified under modern discovery procedures." (Khoury v. Maly's of California (1993) 14 Cal.App.4th 612, 616; see also Lickiss v. Financial Industry Regulatory Authority (2012) 208 Cal.App.4th 1125, 1135 ["the trial court indicated that demurrers for uncertainty are disfavored, and are granted only if the pleading is so incomprehensible that a defendant cannot reasonably respond. We agree"].) Therefore, although 13 does seem to contain a typo, when read against the complaint as a whole, the pleading is not so incomprehensible that Defendant cannot respond.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss On The Grounds Of An Inconvenient Forum, Or In The Alternative For A Stay Of Entire Action
Off Calendar Dec-23-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

DEFENDANT BRADLEY CARRIER's Motion To Dismiss On The Grounds Of An Inconvenient Forum, Or In The Alternative For A Stay Of Entire Action.

Denied. Plaintiff and Defendant's loan agreement contains a mandatory forum selection clause, selecting San Francisco County for all disputes. (Compl., Ex. A, 6.) Thus, Defendant has contracted away his right to choose the forum. (Berg v. MTC Electronics Tch. (1998) 61 Cal.App.4th 349, 358 ["The factors that apply generally to a forum non conveniens motion do not control in a case involving a mandatory forum selection clause."]; Cal-State Bus. Prod. & Servs. (1993) 12 Cal.App.4th 1666, 1683 "[T]he general factors [of forum non conveniens] are pertinent only in the absence of a contractual provision, since under those circumstances neither party possesses a right to any particular forum . . . However, a party which has contracted away its right to choose its home forum . . . may be required to litigate disputes elsewhere."].)

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com
